metadata.summary: SeMPyRO (for Semantic Models Python Pydantic RDF Ontology) is a Pydantic models representing classes of DCAT-AP v3 data model.

documentation:
# SeMPyRO Data Models

**SeMPyRO** (for Semantic Models Python/Pydantic RDF Ontology) is a Python package containing Pydantic models 
representing classes of the DCAT-AP v3 data model. It is designed to streamline data validation, conversion to RDF, 
and schema generation processes.

## Key Features

- **Data Validation**: Easily validate data against schemas, ensuring its correctness, integrity and DCAT-AP compatibility.
- **RDF Graph Conversion**: Convert data into RDF graphs effortlessly, enabling integration with RDF-based systems and applications.
- **Schema Generation**: Generate JSON/YAML schemas compatible with the DCAT-AP v3 profile, facilitating interoperability and compliance with standards.
- **Pedantic Integration**: Built upon the robust [Pydantic](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) library, leveraging its powerful validation capabilities and extending them for RDF handling.

## Background

### DCAT

DCAT is an RDF vocabulary designed to facilitate interoperability between data catalogs published on the Web.

DCAT enables a publisher to describe datasets and data services in a catalog using a standard model and vocabulary
that facilitates the consumption and aggregation of metadata from multiple catalogs. This can increase the
discoverability of datasets and data services. It also makes it possible to have a decentralized approach to publishing
data catalogs and makes federated search for datasets across catalogs in multiple sites possible using the same query
mechanism and structure. Aggregated DCAT metadata can serve as a manifest file as part of the digital preservation
process.

[Official DCAT v3 specification](https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/)

### DCAT-AP

DCAT-AP is a DCAT profile for sharing information about Catalogues containing Datasets and Data Services descriptions
in Europe, under maintenance by the SEMIC action, Interoperable Europe. This Application Profile provides a minimal
common basis within Europe to share Datasets and Data Services cross-border and cross-domain.

[Official DCAT-AP v3 specification](https://semiceu.github.io/DCAT-AP/releases/3.0.0/)

### Health-RI core data model

Health-RI is a no profit organization in the Netherlands dedicated to improving the reuse of health data for policy, 
research, and innovation to achieve a learning healthcare system.
We have published a [Core Metadata Model](https://health-ri.atlassian.net/l/cp/udWLxwpu) based on DCAT-AP 
specification for the sharing of Dutch healthcare-related datasets.
Current package includes Pydantic classes for the **Health-RI Core model**.

## Installation

To install the package run
```commandline
pip install sempyro
```

[Documentation](./docs) folder contains usage examples executable in Jupiter Notebook. 
If you want to explore them follow [official Jupyter installation guid](https://jupyter.org/install#jupyter-notebook).
Additionally you may need to install pandas and/or [erdantic](./docs/Defining_extendind_a_model.md#visualization-with-erdantic).

## Licence

[Apache-2.0](./LICENSE)

## Documentation

For more information on package content and usage please review [documentation](./docs).

resolution, level of detail or profiles (which might specify any or all of the above).

documentation:
A temporal position described using either a (nominal) value from an ordinal reference system,
or a (numeric) value in a temporal coordinate system.

classes.name: sempyro.time.dcat_time_models.GeneralDateTimeDescription
documentation: Description of date and time structured with separate values for the various elements of a calendar-clock system

classes.name: sempyro.time.dcat_time_models.DateTimeDescription

methods.name: sempyro.time.dcat_time_models.DateTimeDescription.force_uriref
documentation:
In case data is provided as json URIRef references are strings, they are converted to URIRef before
 type/values checks on model instantiation

Description of date and time structured with separate values for the various elements of a calendar-clock system.
The temporal reference system is fixed to Gregorian Calendar, and the range of year, month, day properties
restricted to corresponding XML Schema types xsd:gYear, xsd:gMonth and xsd:gDay, respectively.

classes.name: sempyro.time.dcat_time_models.TimeInstant

classes.name: sempyro.spdx.spdx_classes.Checksum
documentation:
A Checksum is value that allows the contents of a file to be authenticated. 
Even small changes to the content of the file will change its checksum. 
This class allows the results of a variety of checksum and cryptographic message digest algorithms to be 
represented.

classes.name: sempyro.prov.prov_classes.Association
documentation:
An activity association is an assignment of responsibility to an agent for an activity, indicating that the agent
had a role in the activity. It further allows for a plan to be specified, which is the plan intended by the agent
to achieve some goals in the context of this activity.

classes.name: sempyro.prov.prov_classes.InstantaneousEvent
documentation:
The PROV data model is implicitly based on a notion of instantaneous events (or just events), that mark
transitions in the world. Events include generation, usage, or invalidation of entities, as well as starting or
ending of activities. This notion of event is not first-class in the data model, but it is useful for explaining
its other concepts and its semantics.

classes.name: sempyro.prov.prov_classes.EntityInfluence
documentation:
EntityInfluence is the capacity of an entity to have an effect on the character, development, or behavior of 
another by means of usage, start, end, derivation, or other.

classes.name: sempyro.prov.prov_classes.End
documentation:
End is when an activity is deemed to have been ended by an entity, known as trigger. The activity no longer exists
after its end. Any usage, generation, or invalidation involving an activity precedes the activity's end.
An end may refer to a trigger entity that terminated the activity, or to an activity, known as ender that generated
the trigger.

classes.name: sempyro.prov.prov_classes.Start
documentation:
Start is when an activity is deemed to have been started by an entity, known as trigger. The activity did
not exist before its start. Any usage, generation, or invalidation involving an activity follows the
activity's start. A start may refer to a trigger entity that set off the activity, or to an activity, known as
starter, that generated the trigger.

classes.name: sempyro.prov.prov_classes.Activity
documentation:
An activity is something that occurs over a period of time and acts upon or with entities; it may include 
consuming, processing, transforming, modifying, relocating, using, or generating entities.

classes.name: sempyro.rdf_model.ModelAnnotationUtil
documentation: A util class for quick access to RDFModel fields info

classes.name: sempyro.rdf_model.LiteralField

methods.name: sempyro.rdf_model.LiteralField.try_solve_datatype
documentation: Tries to find a datatype in rdflib.XSD namespace

Model to handle literal fields
Attributes
----------
datatype : str, pydantic.AnyUrl Optional
    datatype for literal value e.g. 'xsd:date' see https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#built-in-datatypes
language : str Optional
    RFC 3066 language tag, see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3066.html, and also IANA-administrated 
    namespace of language tags: https://www.iana.org/assignments/language-subtag-registry/language-subtag-registry
value : str
    literal value
either datatype or language, or none of these two attributes should be provided 
as per http://www.w3.org/TR/rdf-concepts/#section-Graph-Literal

classes.name: sempyro.rdf_model.RDFModelError
documentation: An error thrown in case of incorrect defined model

classes.name: sempyro.hri_dcat.hri_distribution.HRIDistribution
documentation:
A specific representation of a dataset. A dataset might be available in multiple serializations that may differ
in various ways, including natural language, media-type or format, schematic organization, temporal and spatial
resolution, level of detail or profiles (which might specify any or all of the above).

classes.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.address
documentation: The locn:address property relationship associates any resource with the locn:Address class

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.addressArea
documentation: The name or names of a geographic area or locality that groups a number of addressable objects for addressing purposes, without being an administrative unit. This would typically be part of a city, a neighbourhood or village.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.addressId
documentation: The concept of adding a globally unique identifier for each instance of an address is a crucial part of the INSPIRE data spec.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.adminUnitL1
documentation: Best practice is to use the ISO 3166-1 code but if this is inappropriate for the context, country names should be provided in a consistent manner to reduce ambiguity. For example, either write 'United Kingdom' or 'UK' consistently throughout the data set and avoid mixing the two.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.adminUnitL2
documentation: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of  locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.fullAddress
documentation: The complete address written as a string, with or without formatting.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.geographicName
documentation: A geographic name is a proper noun applied to a spatial object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.geometry
documentation: Associates any resource with the corresponding geometry.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.location
documentation: The location property links any resource to the Location Class. Asserting the location relationship implies only that the domain has some connection to a Location in time or space. It does not imply that the resource is necessarily at that location at the time when the assertion is made.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.locatorDesignator
documentation: A number or a sequence of characters that uniquely identifies the locator within the relevant scope(s). The full identification of the locator could include one or more locator designators.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.locatorName
documentation: Proper noun(s) applied to the real world entity identified by the locator. The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.poBox
documentation: The Post Office Box number.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.postCode
documentation: The post code (a.k.a postal code, zip code etc.). Post codes are common elements in many countries' postal address systems. The domain of locn:postCode is locn:Address.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.postName
documentation: The key postal division of the address, usually the city. (INSPIRE's definition is "One or more names created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.").

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.thoroughfare
documentation: An address component that represents the name of a passage or way through from one location to another. A thoroughfare is not necessarily a road, it might be a waterway or some other feature.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.Address
documentation: An "address representation" as conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Address Representation data type. The locn:addressId property may be used to link this locn:Address to other representations.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.LOCN.LOCN.Geometry
documentation: The locn:Geometry class provides the means to identify a location as a point, line, polygon, etc. expressed using coordinates in some coordinate reference system.

ISA Location Core Vocabulary Namespace
Generated based on https://semiceu.github.io/Core-Location-Vocabulary/releases/w3c/locn.ttl
on 06-02-2024

classes.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.triennial
documentation: The event occurs every three years.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.biennial
documentation: The event occurs every two years.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.annual
documentation: The event occurs once a year.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.semiannual
documentation: The event occurs twice a year.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.threeTimesAYear
documentation: # The event occurs three times a year.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.quarterly
documentation: The event occurs every three months.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.bimonthly
documentation: The event occurs every two months.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.monthly
documentation: The event occurs once a month.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.semimonthly
documentation: The event occurs twice a month.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.biweekly
documentation: The event occurs every two weeks.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.threeTimesAMonth
documentation: The event occurs three times a month.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.weekly
documentation: The event occurs once a week.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.semiweekly
documentation: The event occurs twice a week.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.threeTimesAWeek
documentation: The event occurs three times a week.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.daily
documentation: The event occurs once a day.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.continuous
documentation: The event repeats without interruption.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.FREQ.FREQ.irregular
documentation: The event occurs at uneven intervals.

The Collection Description Frequency Namespace
modified on 2013-05-10

Generated based on https://www.dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/collection-description/frequency/freq.rdf

classes.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.identifier
documentation: Links a resource to an adms:Identifier class.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.includedAsset
documentation: An Asset that is contained in the Asset being described, e.g. when there are several vocabularies defined in a single document.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.interoperabilityLevel
documentation: The interoperability level for which the Asset is relevant.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.last
documentation: A link to the current or latest version of the Asset.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.next
documentation: A link to the next version of the Asset.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.prev
documentation: A link to the previous version of the Asset.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.representationTechnique
documentation: More information about the format in which an Asset Distribution is released. This is different from the file format as, for example, a ZIP file (file format) could contain an XML schema (representation technique).

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.sample
documentation: Links to a sample of an Asset (which is itself an Asset).

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.schemaAgency
documentation: The name of the agency that issued the identifier.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.status
documentation: The status of the Asset in the context of a particular workflow process.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.supportedSchema
documentation: A schema according to which the Asset Repository can provide data about its content, e.g. ADMS.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.translation
documentation: Links Assets that are translations of each other.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.versionNotes
documentation: A description of changes between this version and the previous version of the Asset.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.Asset
documentation: An abstract entity that reflects the intellectual content of the asset and represents those characteristics of the asset that are independent of its physical embodiment. This abstract entity combines the FRBR entities work (a distinct intellectual or artistic creation) and expression (the intellectual or artistic realization of a work)

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.AssetDistribution
documentation: A particular physical embodiment of an Asset, which is an example of the FRBR entity manifestation (the physical embodiment of an expression of a work).

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.AssetRepository
documentation: A system or service that provides facilities for storage and maintenance of descriptions of Assets and Asset Distributions, and functionality that allows users to search and access these descriptions. An Asset Repository will typically contain descriptions of several Assets and related Asset Distributions.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.ADMS.ADMS.Identifier
documentation: This is based on the UN/CEFACT Identifier class.

Namespace for ADMS Vocabulary

ADMS, the Asset Description Metadata Schema, is a vocabulary for describing semantic assets (or just 'Assets'), 
defined as highly reusable metadata (e.g. XML schemata, generic data models) and reference data (e.g. code lists, 
taxonomies, dictionaries, vocabularies).

Generated based on https://uri.semic.eu/w3c/ns/adms.ttl 
Date: 2024-02-26 05

functions.name: sempyro.utils.validator_functions.force_literal_field
documentation:
Converts string values to LiteralField object with none as datatype and language
\:param value: input value
\:param datatype: Optional URIRef
\:return: LiteralField

functions.name: sempyro.utils.validator_functions.date_handler
documentation:
Checks if a string input matches xsd:gYear or xsd:gYearMonth, or can be parsed to a datetime
\:param value: str, input value
\:return: a string if input matches xsd:gYear or xsd:gYearMonth or a {class}`datetime.datetime` object,
non-string inputs are returned unchanged
\:raises: pydantic.ValidationError in case a string can not be parsed to datetime.datetime

classes.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.gmlLiteral
documentation: A GML serialization of a Geometry object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.wktLiteral
documentation: A Well-known Text serialization of a Geometry object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.geoJSONLiteral
documentation: A GeoJSON serialization of a Geometry object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.kmlLiteral
documentation: A KML serialization of a Geometry object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.dggsLiteral
documentation: A textual serialization of a Discrete Global Grid (DGGS) Geometry object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.defaultGeometry
documentation: The default Geometry to be used in spatial calculations. It is usually the most detailed Geometry.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasDefaultGeometry
documentation: The default Geometry to be used in spatial calculations. It is usually the most detailed Geometry.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehContains
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially contains the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*TFF*FF*

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehCoveredBy
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially covered by the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFF*TFT**

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehCovers
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially covers the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*TFT*FF*

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehDisjoint
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially disjoint from the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: FF*FF****

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehEquals
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially equals the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFFFTFFFT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehInside
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially inside the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFF*FFT**

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehMeet
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially meets the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: FT******* ^ F**T***** ^ F***T****

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.ehOverlap
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially overlaps the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*T***T**

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasGeometry
documentation: A spatial representation for a given Feature.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasBoundingBox
documentation: The minimum or smallest bounding or enclosing box of a given Feature.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasCentroid
documentation: The arithmetic mean position of all the Geometry points of a given Feature.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasLength
documentation: The length of a Spatial Object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasPerimeterLength
documentation: The length of the perimeter of a Spatial Object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasArea
documentation: The area of a Spatial Object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasVolume
documentation: The volume of a three-dimensional Spatial Object.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasSpatialResolution
documentation: The spatial resolution of a Geometry.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasSpatialAccuracy
documentation: The positional accuracy of the coordinates of a Geometry.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8dc
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially disjoint from the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: FFTFFTTTT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8ec
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially meets the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: FFTFTTTTT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8eq
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially equals the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFFFTFFFT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8ntpp
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially inside the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFFTFFTTT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8ntppi
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially contains the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TTTFFTFFT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8po
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially overlaps the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TTTTTTTTT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8tpp
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially covered by the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFFTTFTTT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.rcc8tppi
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially covers the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TTTFTTFFT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfContains
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially contains the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*****FF*

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfCrosses
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially crosses the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*T******

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfDisjoint
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially disjoint from the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: FF*FF****

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfEquals
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially equals the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: TFFFTFFFT

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfIntersects
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is not spatially disjoint from the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T******** ^ *T******* ^ ***T***** ^ ****T****

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfOverlaps
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially overlaps the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*T***T**

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfTouches
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject spatially touches the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: FT******* ^ F**T***** ^ F***T****

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.sfWithin
documentation: States that the subject SpatialObject is spatially within the object SpatialObject. DE-9IM: T*F**F***

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasSize
documentation: Subproperties of this property are used to indicate the size of a Spatial Object as a measurement or estimate of one or more dimensions of the Spatial Object's spatial presence.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.asGML
documentation: The GML serialization of a Geometry

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.asWKT
documentation: The WKT serialization of a Geometry

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.asGeoJSON
documentation: The GeoJSON serialization of a Geometry

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.asKML
documentation: The KML serialization of a Geometry

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.asDGGS
documentation: The Discrete Global Grid System (DGGS) serialization of a Geometry

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.coordinateDimension
documentation: The number of measurements or axes needed to describe the position of this Geometry in a coordinate system.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.dimension
documentation: The topological dimension of this geometric object, which must be less than or equal to the coordinate dimension. In non-homogeneous collections, this will return the largest topological dimension of the contained objects.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasSerialization
documentation: Connects a Geometry object with its text-based serialization.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.isEmpty
documentation: (true) if this geometric object is the empty Geometry. If true, then this geometric object represents the empty point set for the coordinate space.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.isSimple
documentation: (true) if this geometric object has no anomalous geometric points, such as self intersection or self tangency.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.spatialDimension
documentation: The number of measurements or axes needed to describe the spatial position of this Geometry in a coordinate system.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricSpatialResolution
documentation: Spatial resolution specifies the level of detail of a Geometry. It the smallest dinstinguishable distance between spatially adjacent coordinates.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricSpatialAccuracy
documentation: The positional accuracy of the coordinates of a Geometry in meters.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricLength
documentation: The length of a Spatial Object in meters.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricPerimeterLength
documentation: The length of the perimeter of a Spatial Object in meters.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricArea
documentation: The area of a Spatial Object in square meters.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricVolume
documentation: The volume of a Spatial Object in cubic meters.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.hasMetricSize
documentation: Subproperties of this property are used to indicate the size of a Spatial Object, as a measurement or estimate of one or more dimensions of the Spatial Object's spatial presence. Units are always metric (meter, square meter or cubic meter).

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.Feature
documentation: A discrete spatial phenomenon in a universe of discourse.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.FeatureCollection
documentation: A collection of individual Features.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.Geometry
documentation: A coherent set of direct positions in space. The positions are held within a Spatial Reference System (SRS).

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.GeometryCollection
documentation: A collection of individual Geometries.

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.SpatialObject
documentation: Anything spatial (being or having a shape, position or an extent).

class_variables.name: sempyro.namespaces.GEOSPARQL.GeoSPARQL.SpatialObjectCollection
documentation: A collection of individual Spatial Objects.

classes.name: sempyro.odrl.policy.ODRLPolicy
documentation: A non-empty group of Permissions and/or Prohibitions.
